candidate.title: Volcano scheduler benchmark and throughput issue slice

identity_keys.title: Volcano scheduler log-level performance incident report
Volcano stable filter skip hot-path issue

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Searched existing local Volcano raw GitHub index and joined-comment corpus under personal-wiki/domains/ai_infra/raw/github/volcano-sh-volcano-closed-issues. No network fetch was used. Issues #999, #1059, #1740, and #5536 are visible in the local index and joined-comment file with scheduler benchmark, scheduler throughput, log-level performance, stable filter, predicateByStablefilter, and PreFilter skip terms.
local_checks.wiki_search: Searched Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure, Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue GitHub Closed Issues, AI Infra Coverage Map, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and prior continuation parent manifests. Parent-5 promoted DRA, ResourceClaimTemplate, PreBind lock, vGPU, Hami vGPU, and device-plugin reinstall boundaries for Volcano #5119, #4692, #5335, #5361, and #2965, but did not promote Volcano #999, #1059, #1740, or #5536 as scheduler benchmark, throughput, log-volume performance, or stable-filter skip evidence.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md routes this work to Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure, Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue GitHub Closed Issues, and AI Infra Coverage Map. No new standalone page was needed.
local_checks.prior_manifest_scan: Parent-18 BlueField-4 and parent-19 Iluvatar Zhikai are hardware catalog slices. Parent-5 is the relevant duplicate boundary for Kubernetes/Kueue/Volcano issue-level orchestration evidence; it intentionally left scheduler benchmark and throughput gaps partial.

duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Before parent-20, the page covered official scheduler/controller/device-plugin/operator surfaces, Kueue admission/preemption/placement examples, Volcano gang scheduling/preemption/vGPU/scheduler panic/upgrade examples, and parent-5 DRA/device-plugin/vGPU/Hami issue signals. It did not contain a Volcano scheduler benchmark-design, throughput-demand, log-volume performance, or stable-filter skip section for #999/#1740/#1059/#5536.
Before parent-20, the corpus page documented counts, raw files, retrieval rules, monthly sync setup, and parent-5 DRA/device-plugin slice. It did not summarize #999, #1059, #1740, or #5536.
Before parent-20, the orchestration-scheduling layer still listed scheduler benchmark evidence as a candidate gap and had no known_source entry for a Volcano benchmark/throughput issue slice.

gap_reason: The local Volcano corpus contains non-duplicate issue-level evidence for scheduler benchmark design, throughput requirements, log-volume performance sensitivity, and stable-filter hot-path skip handling. This improves orchestration-scheduling coverage while preserving the gap for controlled scheduler benchmark results with full environment and measured outputs.

promoted_issue_signals.signal: Benchmark-design request naming scalability performance tests, Kubemark-style node simulation, large pod counts such as 100k pods, 1k-5k nodes, scheduler throughput, average scheduling latency under fixed pod creation frequency, and device-aware repeats such as GPU tests.
promoted_issue_signals.boundary: Design request and pressure-simulator discussion only; no controlled benchmark output was promoted.

promoted_issue_signals.signal: Throughput requirement signal for high-performance workload migration to Kubernetes and large-scale job submission; comments ask for a performance report before further improvement.
promoted_issue_signals.boundary: Requirement and design-pressure evidence only; no throughput number was promoted.

promoted_issue_signals.signal: Reporter-stated performance incident: Volcano v1.0.1 on Kubernetes 1.14 reportedly took nearly 30 minutes to schedule 1k podgroups with 1w pods in a 1w-node cluster, and log growth around 2 MB/s at log level 3 affected scheduler performance.
promoted_issue_signals.boundary: Reporter observation scoped to its stated environment; not a controlled benchmark baseline, production postmortem, SLO, or reproducible result.

promoted_issue_signals.signal: Stable-filter hot-path evidence: predicateByStablefilter needed to honor PreFilter Skip state through handleSkipPredicatePlugin(state, name) before calling Filter for every node.
promoted_issue_signals.boundary: Hot-path optimization evidence only; no measured scheduler throughput or latency output was promoted.

non_promoted_boundaries: No parent-5 DRA, ResourceClaimTemplate, PreBind, vGPU, Hami, device-plugin, Kubernetes DRA, or Kueue DRA issue was re-promoted as new parent-20 evidence.
Volcano #999 and #1740 remain benchmark-design or throughput-request evidence unless future raw sources provide measured results.
Volcano #1059 remains a reporter-stated observation scoped to Volcano v1.0.1 and Kubernetes 1.14, not a controlled benchmark baseline or production postmortem.
Volcano #5536 remains stable-filter hot-path evidence, not proof of measured performance improvement.

No accelerator catalog data, hardware pages, inference runtime pages, data/RAG pages, network/storage pages, crawler code, backend/frontend/dashboard code, source profiles, or subscriptions were changed.
